description_long: This is a vintage gramophone-style record player featuring a large, flared brass horn with ornate embossed patterns radiating from the center. The horn is mounted on a dark, polished wooden base with intricate carved details and a square footprint. A black vinyl record sits atop a circular platter, with a metallic tonearm and crank handle visible on the side. The tonearm is positioned over the record, and the base includes decorative columns and panels. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century phonographs, combining metallic and wooden elements for a classic, antique appearance.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large brass horn, ornate wooden base, and visible vinyl record and tonearm.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the flared brass horn with detailed embossing and the top of the wooden base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length and curve of the horn, the tonearm, crank handle, and the vinyl record on the platter.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the horn, the tonearm's attachment, and the square wooden base with decorative elements.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the horn's profile, the tonearm, and the record, with more detail of the carved wooden base.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and appears to be made of dark material, likely metal or hard plastic, with a black vinyl record placed on top. It is proportionally small compared to the large horn and wooden base, and no distinct grooves or patterns are visible on the platter itself, but the record has a central red label.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and curved, extending from the right side of the base over the record. It is medium in length, silver in color, and appears to have a joint near its base for movement. The tonearm is positioned over the platter, suggesting it is ready to play.
